page: Amazon Web Services r5b.large is a memory-optimized fifth-generation server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. The instance provides 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, yielding 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the r5b.large in the lower performance tiers for compute and memory throughput. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is weak, while multi-core CPU performance falls into the poor category. Memory bandwidth tests show poor results, with read-only large block speeds at 12.97 GB/sec and write large block speeds at 11.36 GB/sec. Geekbench scores reflect an average single-core performance but poor multi-core performance. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the bottom 10 percent.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5b.large offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio on dedicated hardware, making it a viable entry point for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive CPU processing. However, the tradeoff is its limited compute capacity and low network baseline of 1 Gbps. This instance is best suited for small-scale database operations, caching layers, lightweight memory-intensive applications, and basic web serving where memory capacity is prioritized over raw computational speed.
description: Amazon Web Services r5b.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, it utilizes the Nitro hypervisor. The CPU features a 35 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 instructions. The instance lacks local storage and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates weak single-core CPU performance and poor multi-core and memory bandwidth results, though Geekbench single-core performance is average. With an eight-to-one memory-to-vCPU ratio, the r5b.large offers a qualitative cost-effective entry point for memory-bound tasks that do not demand high compute power. It is best suited for small-scale database operations, caching layers, and lightweight memory-intensive applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5b.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8259CL CPU, designed for entry-level memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5b.large is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU for memory-bound workloads.
tagline: This dedicated memory optimized instance provides a high memory to processor ratio for entry level workloads requiring significant memory capacity.

Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and no local storage
Average single-core performance with lower multi-core and memory bandwidth
